channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I cut Python JSON memory overhead from 1.9GB to ~0MB (11x Speedup)
item.description: The Problem: The "PyObject" TaxWe all love Python for its developer velocity, but for high-scale data engineering, the interpreter's overhead is a silent killer.I was recently benchmarking...

item.title: Golang G/M/P Time Scale
item.description: If we imagine that 1 millisecond is one “day”, then 1 second becomes about 3 years. 1 s ≈ 3 “years” 1 ms = 1 “day” 2 µs ≈ 1 “minute” 10 ns ≈ 1 “second” Thus, the RTT over 5000 km (~56 ms) is about...

item.title: Securing a Public API: Using AWS Best Practices
item.description: `` ## I have a React web application hosted on AWS (e.g., using Amazon S3 and CloudFront) that utilizes a Python API deployed on AWS (e.g., via Amazon ECS, AWS Lambda, or EC2 behind API Gateway)....

item.title: AS’ HTCPCP AI Butler™ — The AI That Brews Chaos, Not Coffee 418% Chaos: Your Useless AI Butler
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge 🍵 AS’ HTCPCP AI Butler™ 🤖 ... The Most Overengineered Useless AI Ever Created This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge 🍵 AS’...

item.title: Your Architecture Diagram Is Lying To You
item.description: The 30-Minute Lie Last month, I spent 30 minutes in Lucidchart before a design review. I dragged every box. Connected every arrow. Labeled every subnet, every security group, every load balancer....

item.title: Method Overloading in Java
item.description: Method Overloading is a feature in Java where multiple methods have the same name but different parameters. It helps improve code readability and allows you to use the same method name for...

item.title: Methods Examples in Java
item.description: Void method(With return) public class Display{ public static void main(String[] args){ Display casio = new Display(); casio.add(); } public void add(){ return; } } Step-by-Step Execution Program...

item.title: Synchronous, asynchronous, concurrent, parallel: the 4 concepts explained in Go
item.description: These four words show up everywhere in docs, articles, and interviews. And they get mixed up constantly — including by experienced developers. "Asynchronous" and "concurrent" don't mean the same...

item.title: String Manipulation in Python
item.description: What is a String? A string is a sequence of characters used to store text in a program. Characters can include: Letters (A–Z, a–z) Numbers (0–9) Symbols (@, #, !, etc.) Spaces 1. Accessing...

item.title: Scan MCP tool definitions for prompt injection before your agent calls them
item.description: MCP servers expose tools to AI agents. But those tool definitions can contain prompt injection, typosquatted names, hidden unicode, and hardcoded secrets. Your agent trusts whatever tool...

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known as Contagious Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's...

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs),...

item.title: Nothing’s noise-canceling CMF Buds 2A are down to $19.99 for the rest of today
item.description: It’s not every day you find a decent pair of wireless earbuds with active noise cancellation, a transparency mode, and app support for less than $20, which is why the current lighting deal on the...

item.title: Amazon is ending support for older Kindles and Kindle Fires
item.description: Amazon has announced that starting on May 20th, 2026, Kindle e-readers and Kindle Fire devices released in 2012 and earlier will "no longer be able to purchase, borrow, or download new content via...

item.title: I can’t help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee
item.description: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

item.title: Spotify’s Prompted Playlists can help you find new podcasts to listen to
item.description: On Tuesday, Spotify expanded its Prompted Playlists feature to include podcasts, an update that could make it easier for Premium users to find new shows to listen to. Prompted Playlists were...

item.title: You can now turn 2D apps into 3D while using the Galaxy XR headset
item.description: Android XR is getting a new feature that turns 2D apps, websites, images, and videos into "3D experiences." The feature, which Google calls "auto-spatialization," was initially announced last...

item.title: I wish this selfie phone case was better for selfies
item.description: The Selfix phone case is a classic example of a great idea in theory that falls apart in practice. It's a case for the iPhone 17 Pro with a circular screen on the back. It mirrors the middle of...

item.title: Anthropic Teams Up With Its Rivals to Keep AI From Hacking Everything
item.description: The AI lab's Project Glasswing will bring together Apple, Google, and more than 45 other organizations. They'll use the new Claude Mythos Preview model to test advancing AI cybersecurity capabilities.

item.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
item.description: Nvidia-backed Asia AI data center provider Firmus has now raised $1.35 billion in six months.

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy S26 Ultra is $200 off for the first time
item.description: My colleague Allison Johnson loved the Privacy Display in her review of the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra. The display feature, which blocks onlookers from getting a peep at what’s on the screen when...

item.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project
item.description: Intel will join SpaceX and Tesla in an effort to build a new U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, although the scope of its contributions are unclear.

item.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
item.description: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

item.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems ‘in every major operating system and web browser’
item.description: Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it's called, is...

item.title: Cybersecurity in the Age of Instant Software
item.description: AI is rapidly changing how software is written, deployed, and used. Trends point to a future where AIs can write custom software quickly and easily: “instant software.” Taken to an extreme, it...

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned...

item.title: The rugged Bose Soundlink Flex is 25 percent off right now
item.description: We saw a host of excellent Bose discounts during Amazon’s so-called Big Spring Sale last month; however, none of them were quite as steep as the current price drop we’re seeing on the latest Bose...

item.title: Vertical browser tabs are better and you should use them
item.description: Google's Chrome browser is getting a couple of new features, both of them extremely welcome and wildly overdue. The first is a reading mode, which does what it already does in most other browsers:...

item.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description: The Russia-linked threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard) has been linked to a new campaign that has compromised insecure MikroTik and TP-Link routers and modified their settings to turn...
